Amendments1997—Subsec. (c). substituted “if there is such proof of exportation as the Secretary may by regulations require.” for “on the submission of such evidence, records, and certification indicating exportation as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe.”Pub. L. 105–341979—Subsec. (a). , struck out “for storage therein pending exportation” after “customs bonded warehouse”.Pub. L. 96–39, § 807(a)(15)(A)Subsec. (b). , substituted “from bonded premises by the proprietor for exportation without payment of tax, the bond of such proprietor required to be furnished under paragraph (1) of section 5173(a) covering such premises shall cover such exportation, and subsection (a) shall not apply” for “for exportation without payment of tax on application of the proprietor of bonded premises, the bond of such proprietor covering such bonded premises shall cover such exportation and subsection (a) shall not be applicable”.Pub. L. 96–39, § 807(a)(15)(B)1977—Subsec. (a). required export bonds for withdrawals from bonded premises, without payment of tax, for transfer to a customs bonded warehouse for storage therein pending exportation.Pub. Continuation of Distiller’s Notice and Bond, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 85–859, title II, § 210(f)72 Stat. 1436Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Sept. 2, 1958Oct. 22, 1986“Notwithstanding any provision of section 5175 or 5176(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954], the Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate may waive, as to registered distillers or registered fruit distillers qualified to operate under bond on , requirements for filing notice and executing new bond on , if the distiller and the surety have executed consent to continuation of the terms of the existing bond to cover operations from , to , both dates inclusive. Nothing in this subsection shall be construed as limiting the authority of the Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate under section 5176(b) or (c) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.”April 30, 1959May 1, 1959May 1, 1959June 30, 1959

children.heading: Requirements
children.label: a Requirements
children.text: No distilled spirits shall be withdrawn from bonded premises for exportation, or for transfer to a customs bonded warehouse, without payment of tax unless the exporter has furnished bond to cover such withdrawal under such regulations and conditions, and in such form and penal sum, as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.heading: Exception where proprietor withdraws spirits for exportation
children.label: b Exception where proprietor withdraws spirits for exportation
children.text: In the case of distilled spirits withdrawn from bonded premises by the proprietor for exportation without payment of tax, the bond of such proprietor required to be furnished under paragraph (1) of section 5173(a) covering such premises shall cover such exportation, and subsection (a) shall not apply.

children.heading: Cancellation or credit of export bonds
children.label: c Cancellation or credit of export bonds
children.text: The bonds given under subsection (a) shall be cancelled or credited and the bonds liable under subsection (b) credited if there is such proof of exportation as the Secretary may by regulations require.
